You are an a11y QA reviewer testing keyboard focus visibility specifically on DARK-background controls (the bug class is focus indicator becoming invisible against dark themes). Budget: 8 turns.

CONTEXT: at the top of the Adobe resources page there are two hero promo cards. The right one ("ASSESSMENT — Customer Experience AI Maturity Index") has a RED/dark background with a "Start now" button on it. That is the kind of element where mwpw-190906 class bugs hide.

STEP A: Bring the hero promo cards into view. (1 turn)
- Navigate.
- find_and_show selector "[class*=\"merch-card\"], [class*=\"hero\"], [class*=\"promo\"], button:has-text(\"Start now\"), button:has-text(\"Read now\"), a:has-text(\"Start now\"), a:has-text(\"Read now\")".
- If that misses, fallback: evaluate({code: window.scrollTo(0, 200)}) and take_screenshot.

STEP B: Focus a button on a dark/colored background. (2-3 turns)
- Use get_interactives (no scope, walk whole page) and find a ref whose label matches "Start now", "Read now", "Get started", "Learn more", or similar CTA on a darker background.
- click is NOT what we want — instead, use evaluate to .focus() that ref's element directly:
  evaluate({code: `
    const el = window.__qaMap.get(REF_NUMBER);
    if (el) el.focus();
    'focused: ' + (el && (el.textContent||'').trim().slice(0,40));
  `})
  (Replace REF_NUMBER with the actual ref integer.)
- take_screenshot labeled "focus_dark_bg".

STEP C: Inspect computed focus styling. (1 turn)
- evaluate({code: `JSON.stringify((() => {
    const el = document.activeElement;
    if (!el) return {none: true};
    const s = getComputedStyle(el);
    const parent = el.closest('[class*="card"], [class*="hero"], [class*="promo"], section, div');
    const parentStyle = parent ? getComputedStyle(parent) : null;
    return {
      tag: el.tagName,
      label: (el.textContent||el.getAttribute('aria-label')||'').trim().slice(0,60),
      focusStyle: { outlineStyle: s.outlineStyle, outlineColor: s.outlineColor, outlineWidth: s.outlineWidth, boxShadow: s.boxShadow.slice(0,150), border: s.border, color: s.color, backgroundColor: s.backgroundColor },
      parentBackground: parentStyle && { backgroundColor: parentStyle.backgroundColor, backgroundImage: parentStyle.backgroundImage.slice(0,80) }
    };
  })())`})

STEP D: done(report, verdict).

VERDICT — both must pass:
- VISUAL: looking at the focus_dark_bg screenshot, you can clearly see a focus indicator on the targeted button against its dark/colored background. The indicator is NOT washed out, NOT the same color as the background, NOT a low-contrast outline against a similar-colored surface.
- COMPUTED: the focused element has a focus indicator (outlineStyle != "none" with non-transparent color OR a visible boxShadow). If the indicator IS present but its color is too close to the parent's background color, that's a fail too.
- PASS if focus is unmistakably visible against the dark background. FAIL if focus is invisible, washed out, or low-contrast against the parent background (mwpw-190906 / mwpw-190007 class).

REPORT
- Which element you focused, what its parent background was (color value), what the focus indicator was (outline/boxShadow), whether the indicator was distinguishable against that background, verdict reason.
